methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Persona AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Nic Radford and Jerry Pratt that has raised $25M to date. The broader sector comprises 9 peer companies—1 public and 8 private—including firms such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing sector to support a reliable peer-median valuation approach; applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

Stage-progression analysis, the applicable method here, uses the $25M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage step-ups in venture markets. This yields an implied valuation of $62M. This method assumes standard dilution and step-up dynamics hold for Persona AI and that raised capital reasonably proxies for current valuation in the absence of independent pricing data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $53M to $72M, with a median of $62M. Given reliance on a single methodological input, key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier to Persona AI's specific capital efficiency and market conditions, the absence of peer benchmarking to validate sector-specific step-up patterns, and any material operational or fundraising developments since the $25M raise. Additional data on recent financing rounds or comparable seed-stage robotics exits would improve conviction.
